round0_outcome.note: Round-0 ran ONE measurement family (depth_reach: depth_lift / winner_deeper) for both hypotheses. 14 branches cleared depth_lift>=2.0 & winner_deeper (the iteration-/path-depth subtype is REAL and confirmed). The other 39 were scored (n_decidable=2, n_scorable=2) and FAILED the depth rule -> rule_not_met. Round-2 does NOT re-litigate the 14; it asks whether the 39 carry a SECOND, corpus-scale ctx mechanism a different BUILT tool can confirm, and otherwise marks them honestly inconclusive.

hypotheses.label: naive_ctx's resolving corpus drives the blocker line DEEPER per seed (more loop/recursion/path re-entries, or actually takes the blocked side) than naive's blocking corpus
hypotheses.mechanism: CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so each loop re-entry / recursion depth level / distinct caller chain at the blocker registers as a fresh coverage bucket. This manufactures a depth gradient that retains seeds driving the blocker line to higher execution counts (and/or taking the blocked side) than naive's context-blind, saturated edge map. This is the per-seed-MEASURABLE face of the ctx advantage: the winner corpus reaches the blocker line deeper. Round-0 confirmed it for 14 branches; this is the SAME rule, kept verbatim so the 14 stay validated and any further branch that genuinely clears the bar is caught.
hypotheses.prediction: naive_ctx's saved corpus drives the blocker line to a strictly higher per-seed execution count than naive's (depth_lift >= 2.0 AND winner_deeper == True). A value/byte-solving mechanism mis-bucketed into this naive_ctx>naive shape would reach the blocker line at the SAME depth and differ only in operand bytes -> depth_lift ~ 1.0 -> rule fails. A reversed depth (naive deeper, depth_lift < 1, winner_deeper == False, observed e.g. harfbuzz/5237 = 0.14) also fails. The rule is therefore falsifiable and discriminating: it confirms a depth/path-iteration advantage and rejects a flat or reversed depth profile.

hypotheses.label: naive_ctx hoards far more (context-redundant) saved-corpus entries than naive — a whole-campaign corpus-scale advantage independent of any single seed's terminal depth
hypotheses.mechanism: ctx_coverage is a NON-LOCAL feedback: its advantage is supposed to be that the (call-context, edge) map admits many more seeds — the same edge reached through a new caller chain counts as new coverage, so naive_ctx retains a much larger, more context-diverse corpus than naive's saturated bare-edge map. If that hoarding is real, the corpus-SCALE signature is visible whether or not any single terminal seed reaches the blocker deeper: a higher saved-corpus entry count for the naive_ctx arm. This is the second, corpus-scale face of the mechanism that depth_reach (per-seed terminal depth) is structurally blind to. It is arm-level (branch-independent): one (target, naive_ctx, naive) measurement labels every branch of that target.

params.corpus_count_ratio: median(#saved seeds naive_ctx) / median(#saved seeds naive) across trials

hypotheses.prediction: If ctx hoards a larger context-diverse corpus, the naive_ctx arm saves strictly MORE entries than naive (corpus_count_ratio >= 1.5). A 'no corpus-scale effect' alternative predicts ratio ~ 1.0, and a corpus-SHRINKING arm predicts ratio < 1. MEASURED on this shape's s4 targets: harfbuzz corpus_count_ratio = 0.924, sqlite3 = 0.956 (entropy ratios 1.016 / 1.011 — no homogenization either). So for harfbuzz and sqlite3 the corpus-inflation mechanism is REFUTED by the data — naive_ctx does NOT hoard more seeds. The rule is retained as a genuine falsifiable second-mechanism test (it CAN fire >=1.5 on a target where ctx truly inflates), but on the measured arms it fails, which is the correct, honest outcome — it does NOT rescue the 39 into a label.

decidable_false_subgroup.label: non_local_ctx_no_seed_scale_signature
decidable_false_subgroup.criterion: branches that fail BOTH rules above: depth_lift < 2.0 (per-seed terminal depth does not separate) AND corpus_count_ratio < 1.5 (the arm does not hoard a larger corpus)
decidable_false_subgroup.rationale: ctx_coverage's advantage on these branches operated at whole-campaign / scheduling scale (which seeds got energy, when) and left NO terminal corpus signature a built tool can read: the winner corpus neither reaches the blocker line deeper per seed nor is larger overall. This is exactly the spec's expected non-local / non-synthesizable case (guardrail G3). These branches are honest decidable:false / inconclusive, NOT a missing mechanism to be invented.
decidable_false_subgroup.note: decidable:false is recorded only where BOTH built tools actually ran and returned a measurement (n_scorable=2). It is NOT used merely because a target's corpus lives on the other server — every ctx target's naive_ctx/naive corpus exists on its owning server (s4: harfbuzz/sqlite3; sB: libpng/libxml2/lcms), so both tools are scorable on both servers.

notes.what_changed_from_round0: Round-0 effectively ran only depth_reach (both its hypotheses collapsed to the depth_lift/winner_deeper rule the arbiter applied). It validated 14 branches and left 39 inconclusive as rule_not_met. Round-2 keeps depth_reach VERBATIM as hypothesis 1 (so the 14 stay validated — do not re-test them) and ADDS the one genuinely-different BUILT tool that targets the NON-LOCAL face depth_reach is blind to: corpus_size_ratio (ctx_corpus_inflation). No new tool is proposed; both registry_tools are already built (bench/tools/depth_reach.py, bench/tools/corpus_size_ratio.py).
notes.second_mechanism_outcome: I directly RAN corpus_size_ratio on this shape's s4 targets: harfbuzz corpus_count_ratio = 0.924, sqlite3 = 0.956 (entropy ratios ~1.01). Both are BELOW 1.0 — naive_ctx's saved corpus is slightly SMALLER, not larger. The corpus-inflation hypothesis is therefore REFUTED for harfbuzz/sqlite3, not confirmed. It is kept in the test as a falsifiable rule (it would fire on a target where ctx truly hoards) but it will NOT relabel the inconclusive branches on these arms. This is the correct G3 outcome: I tested a discriminating alternative with a built tool and the data said no.
notes.depth_near_miss_check: I re-ran depth_reach on a sample of the inconclusive harfbuzz branches to confirm they are genuine failures, not a rescuable near-miss cohort: 5237 depth_lift=0.14 (winner_deeper=False, naive reaches it DEEPER), 5990 depth_lift=1.02 (flat, the textbook 'no corpus effect' profile), 5878 depth_lift=1.52 (a thin near-miss clearing 1.5 but not 2.0). The signal degrades smoothly toward 1.0/<1.0 across the inconclusive set. I deliberately did NOT lower the threshold to 1.5 to harvest near-misses like 5878: a 1.5x per-seed depth bump with no blocked-side advantage is not a confident mechanism confirmation, and dropping below the 2.0 bar that the 14 validated branches cleared would admit noise and contaminate the labeled cohort. Holding the bar keeps the validated set clean and the borderline branches honestly inconclusive.
notes.expected_split: validated (depth subtype, depth_lift>=2.0 & winner_deeper): the 14 round-0 branches — harfbuzz {5503,6061,6093,6539,6585,6613,6616}, libpng {3872,3965,3996}, libxml2 {6433,6467,6597,6674}. decidable:false / inconclusive (fail both rules): the remaining 39 — harfbuzz {5237,5302,5517,5708,5839,5878,5887,5990,6130}, sqlite3 {14535,14745,14762,15434}, libpng {3864,3874,3896,3951,3966,3980,4032,4039,4042,4055,4061,4085}, libxml2 {12282,6405,6483,6581,6616,6674-no(6674 validated),6887,6924,6925,6926,6948,7476,7783,8630}, lcms {2342}. (Exact per-branch labels are the arbiter's; this is the design-stage expectation.)
notes.G1_falsifiability: Both rules can come out the other way and rule out a concrete alternative. iteration_path_depth_reach: a value/byte mechanism mis-bucketed here predicts depth_lift~1.0 (same line depth, differ only in bytes) -> fails; observed reversals (5237=0.14) and flats (5990=1.02) DO fail it, proving it is not auto-true. ctx_corpus_inflation: a no-inflation alternative predicts ratio~1.0; the measured 0.924/0.956 fail it -> proving it is not auto-true and that I am reporting a refutation, not a confirmation.
notes.G2_independence: Both measurements are independent of the resolve pattern that defined the shape (naive_ctx-resolves / naive-blocks, a coverage OUTCOME). depth_reach measures HOW DEEP each arm's corpus drives the blocker LINE; corpus_size_ratio measures HOW MANY entries each arm SAVED. Neither reads the resolve label; we never 'verify naive_ctx resolves by observing naive_ctx resolves'.
notes.data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage / mutation_op events (the ctx effect is a feedback/corpus property, invisible in mutation_op). Both tools read saved corpora / replay seeds, which exist on each target's OWNING server (s4: harfbuzz, sqlite3; sB: libpng, libxml2, lcms). The sB round-0 assignments confirm depth_reach already scored libpng/libxml2/lcms there (n_scorable=2 with real metrics), so corpus_size_ratio (same corpus source) is equally scorable on sB. decidable:false is therefore an EVIDENCE-BACKED 'both tools ran and said no', NOT an off-server skip.
